original: St Sergius I reign as Catholic Pope ends
Treaty of Neuberg splits the Austrian Habsburg lands between the Habsburg Dukes Albert III and Leopold III
Battle of Krbava Field: Ottoman Empire decisively defeats an army of the Kingdom of Croatia
The Roman Inquisition burns all copies of the Talmud and other Jewish texts in Rome's Campo de Fiori
Dutch leaders Lomaraal, Count of  Egmont and Philip van Hoorne arrested by Spanish Duke of Alba - their execution in 1568 leads to the Eighty Years' War
Cyprian city of Nicosia falls to the Ottomans, afterwards an estimated 20,000 citizen are massacred and the rest sold into slavery
Sinking of the English ship Squirrel off the Azores with explorer and founder of Newfoundland, Humphrey Gilbert, on board
-10] Battle at Flores Azores: Spanish beat English
New England colonies declare war on Wampanoag Indians
Expelled Polish & Lotharings reach Wienerwald
Stono slave rebellion, South Carolina: 60 slaves kill about 20 white people before being killed or later executed. Largest slave uprising in British mainland colonies before American Revolution
1st steam engine arrives in North American colonies
Colegio de San Ignacio de Loyola Vizcaínas - the oldest continuously operating educational institution in Mexico, opens for the education of girls and women in Mexico City
Congress officially renames the country as the United States of America (from the United Colonies)
Alexander Twilight, likely the first African American to graduate from a US college, receives a BA degree from Middlebury College
Charles Durant, 1st US aeronaut, flies a balloon from Castle Garden, NYC to Perth Amboy, NJ, a dsstance of about 25 miles, covered in 3 hours
Great Lakes steamer "Erie" sinks off Silver Creek NY, kills 300
Tom Hyer beats George McChester in 101 rounds at Caldwell's Landing, NY to become first American heavyweight boxing champion
California is admitted as the thirty-first state of the Union
Territories of New Mexico & Utah created
Nurse Sally Tompkins is officially commissioned as an officer (and its only female officer) by the Confederate States Army
Battle of Cumberland Gap, Tennessee
Luxembourg gains its independence
Lotta's Fountain (corner Kearny & Market) dedicated in San Francisco
PresidentRutherford B. Hayesvisits San Francisco
Egyptian military coup under colonel Arab "El Wahid"
The Berne Convention for the Protection of Literary and Artistic Works is finalized
Edward Emerson Barnard at Lick Observatory discovers Amalthea, Jupiter's 5th moon
Manifesto of the Queensland Labour Party to the people of Queensland issued, detailing grievances of working class towards ruling class. Pivotal document in Australian labor and political history.
American Bowling Congress forms (NYC)
Ottawa Football Club re-organizes into the Rough Riders
6 km long Engadin-railroad tunnel in Switzerland inaugurated
Boston Herald again refers to NY baseball club as Yankees, when it reports "Yankees take 2," Yankee name not official till 1913
Mounted police first appear in NYC
Russia annexes part of Poland
China's Metropolitan Library established by the Qing Dynasty in Beijing Guanghua temple (now the National Library of China)
1st European post delivered by air (Hendon to Windsor, England)
French aviator Jules Védrines is the first to fly over 100 mph (107 mph/172 kph)

Imperial Russian Air Service pilot Lt Pyotr Nesterov (26), performs full aerial loop in his Nieuport IV monoplane over Syretzk Aerodrome near Kiev; initially punished for risking government equipment, he was later lauded for his innovation  [27 August O.S.]
-12] Belgian offensive from Antwerp
Boston Brave George Davis no-hits Philadelphia Phillies, 7-0
First fully mechanized unit in the British Army created - the Canadian Automobile Machine Gun Brigade (WWI)
Meeting held at Gaelic League headquarters between Irish Republican Brotherhood and other extreme republicans; initial decision made to stage an uprising while Britain is at war
Association for the Study of Negro Life and History formed in Chicago by Carter G. Woodson and others. Now the Association for the Study of African American Life and History.
Dutch government of Ruijs de Beerenbrouck forms
Boston's police force goes on strike
Guatemala, Honduras and San Salvador agree to Central American Union
St Louis Brown "Baby Doll" Jacobson hits 3 triples beating Tigers 16-0
Turkish troops take the Greek-held Anatolian city of Smyrna during the Greco-Turkish War
Albin Stenroos runs world record 20 km (1:07:11.2)
Hanapepe Massacre occurs on Kauai, Hawaii
National Broadcasting Company created by Radio Corporation of America
Train disaster at Wassenaar, Netherlands, 4 die
Silvio Cator of Haiti, sets then long jump record at 26' ½"
Frank Crosetti ties record, striking out twice in 1 inning
Mine strikes at Belgian Borinage ends
Spanish Cortes grants Catalonia autonomy
Steamboat SS Observation explodes in NYC East River with 71 killed
New York Yankees beat Cleveland Indians, 12-9 at League Park to clinch AL pennant on the earliest date in history
Nazi army reaches Warsaw
28 German aircraft shot down above England
1st bombing on continental US soil at Mount Emily, Oregon during WWII by Japanese planes
Compulsory work for women, children and old males in Batavia
15 German JU-88's sink Italian flag ship Rome
US, British & French troops land in Salerno (operation Avalanche)
Allied forces liberate Luxembourg
Dutch Resistance fighter Jaap Musch executed after a day and a half of torture and interrogation regarding his work saving Jewish children in Nijverdal, Netherlands
Red Army supports coup in Bulgaria, instituting new Communist government (1946-1990) during the "National Uprising"
US 113th cavalry passes Belgian-Dutch borders
Japanese in South Korea, Taiwan, China, Indochina surrender to Allies
Philadelphia A's Dick Fowler no-hits St Louis Browns, 1-0
Brooklyn Dodger Rex Barney no-hits NY Giants, 2-0
Pacific Airlines flight explodes en route to Baie-Comeau, killing 23 people. A passenger's husband Albert Guay later sentenced and hanged along with two others for first bombing of a Canadian plane
1st use of TV laugh track by "The Hank McCune Show" in the US
Mass arrests of communists in France
First broadcast of the soap opera "Love of Life" on CBS TV
WFIE TV channel 14 in Evansville, IN (NBC) begins broadcasting
Earthquake strikes Orleansville (now Chlef), Algeria: 1,250 killed with many aftershocks
Indians becomes 1st Cleveland team to win 100 games in a season
Don Zimmer, hits 4,000th Dodger home run
African Party for Liberation of Guinea-Bissau & Cape Verde forms
Nashville's new Hattie Cotton Elementary School dynamited
Race riots in Notting Hill Gate, London
Denver Broncos beat Boston Patriots, 13-10 in the American Football League's first game before 21,597 fans at Nickerson Field in Boston
Pakistan ends India's run of 6 consecutive Olympic field hockey gold medals with a 1-0 win over their sub-continent rivals at the Rome Games
Soviet economist Liberman plead for autonomous businesses
WNYS (now WIXT) TV channel 9 in Syracuse, NY (ABC) begins broadcasting
NBC expands "The Huntley–Brinkley Report", their evening television news program, from 15 to 30 minutes
German DR government allows short visits to West Germany
John Osborne's "Inadmissible Evidence" premieres in London
Tibet is made an autonomous region of China
1st successful Test flight of a Saturn V
Uganda declares independence from Great Britain
WGIQ TV channel 43 in Louisville, AL (PBS) begins broadcasting
WVPT TV channel 51 in Staunton, VA (PBS) begins broadcasting
Allegheny Airlines flight 853 collides with a Piper Cherokee above Indiana, kills all 83 occupants
The Official Languages Act comes into force in Canada - making English and French the country's official languages (replaced 1988 by new Official Languages Act)
WCVN TV channel 54 in Covington, KY (PBS) begins broadcasting
Feyenoord wins soccer's Club World Cup
1,000 convicts riot and seize Attica Prison in New York
John Lennonand Yoko Ono appear on Dick Cavett Show (ABC-TV)
NHL great Gordie Howe retires (for the first time), after 25 seasons with the Detroit Red Wings
Connection found between Mammoth Cave Ridge and Flint cave systems in Kentucky, joining 144 miles of passages - making it the world's longest known cave system (later mapped at 420 miles)
Soviet runner Lyudmila Bragina with a world record 4:01.38 wins the women's 1,500m gold medal at the Munich Olympics

Soviet Union beats the United States 51-50 in the most controversial game in international basketball history; with the US leading 50-49, the final 3 seconds are replayed 3 times until the Soviets finally win
West German equestrian rider Liselott Linsenhoff follows her dressage teams gold in Mexico City with the individual dressage title at her home Olympics in Munich
Viking 2 Mars probe launches

New Zealand government establishes the country’s first centralised electronic database through the Wanganui Computer Act, raising questions about the state’s ability to gather information on its citizens
Tiger rookies Lou Whitaker & Allan Trammell debut together & will play together for 19 years
3rd game of Boston Massacre; NY Yankees beat Red Sox, 7-0
Baltimore Orioles pull their 7th triple play (5-4-3 vs Toronto)
31st Emmy Awards: "Taxi"; "Lou Grant"; Ron Leibman & Ruth Gordon win
South African Ferrari driver Jody Scheckter wins the Italian Grand Prix at Monza to clinch his first Formula 1 World Drivers Championship; first RSA champion
Vernon E. Jordan resigns as president of National Urban League in the US
Columbia mated with SRBs & external tank in preparation for STS-5
Conestoga 1, 1st private commercial rocket, makes suborbital flight
Challenger returns to Kennedy Space Center via Sheppard AFB, Texas
Radio Shack announces their color computer 2 (Coco2)
Vitas Gerulaitis bets his house thatMartina Navratilovacan't beat 100th ranked male tennis player
Race riot in Birmingham, England
Minn Tommy Krammer passes for 6 touchdowns vs Green Bay (42-7)
NYC jury indicts Soviet United Nations employee Gennadly Zakharov of spying
Gary Hart admits on "Nightline" to cheating on his wife
"Look Away" single released by Chicago (Billboard Song of the Year 1989)
MLB Atlanta Braves Bruce Sutter joins Rollie Fingers & Gooses Gossage with 300 MLB career saves
US Stars & Stripes H3 defeats NZ's KZ-1 at the 27th America's Cup: NZ appeal in court but eventually lose
George H. W. Bush and Mikhail Gorbachev meet in Helsinki & urge Iraq to leave Kuwait
Liberia president Samuel K Doe is captured by Mr Johnson's forces
Oakland beats NY 7-3 to complete a 12-game sweep of Yankees this year
Only 1,695 fans watch Boston Red Sox play Cleveland
Baltimore Orioles draw 3 million fans at home for the first time
MLB player Robin Yount, is 17th to reach 3,000 hits
Croupier of casino in Bristol, England, shoots a 4 a record eight times
Israel and the Palestine Liberation Organization exchange letters of mutual recognition
Russian Tu-22 supersonic bomber collides with its photographic chase aircraft, a Tu-134AK, over Samoyliha, Shatura District near Moscow, killing all eight aboard the Tu-134
Space shuttle STS 64 (Discovery 20) launches into orbit
"Broadway Limited" last train ride (began in 1902)
Dean St Station in Brooklyn, is 6th MTA station to close since 1904
Sinn Féin accepts Mitchell Principles on para-military disarmament
At 01:46:40 UTC the time on the Unix clock in milliseconds passes 1 billion since January 1, 1970, which Unix systems recognize as zero-time
MLB Arizona Diamondbacks' lefthanded pitcher Randy Johnson reaches 300 strikeouts for the fifth consecutive season, extending his major league record
Bomb explodes outside the Australian embassy in Jakarta, killing 10 people
Cyprus enters recession after 0.6% contraction in the first quarter, followed by 0.4% in the second quarter
100 people are killed and 350 injured after a wave of attacks across Iraq
17 people are killed and at least 40 injured after two car bombs explode in Aleppo, Syria
Armenia wins the 40th FIDE Chess Olympiad
18 people are killed in conflict between government and Boko Harem troops in Borno State, Nigeria
44 people are killed and 45 are injured after a bus crashes into a ravine in Northern Guatemala
60 people are killed in conflict between rebels and loyalists in the Central African Republic
Erna Solberg is elected Prime Minister of Norway after a centre-right coalition wins a majority in their elections
Ali Hosseini Khamenei, Supreme Leader of Iran, undergoes prostate surgery
Apple unveils the iPad Pro and iPhone 6S in San Francisco
EU Migrant Crisis: European Commission President Jean-Claude Juncker in his annual address proposes plan based on EU quotas
North Korea conducts its fifth nuclear test at the Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site, at the time its largest ever test at 10 kilotons but superseded by the 2017 test
Egyptian archaeologists announce the discovery of a 3,500-year-old tomb of a goldsmith and his family in Draa Abul-Naga, Egypt
Arirang Mass Games begins in North Korea to mark country's 70th anniversary featuring tens of thousands performing
CBS chief Les Moonves departs the company after six more women make allegations of sexual abuse in "The New Yorker"
Green Bay Packers start 100th season with historic 24-23 comeback win over Chicago Bears at Lambeau Field; first ever Packer recovery from 17+ points deficit at 3/4 time (20-3)
Russian police detain over 1000 people amid nationwide protests against pension reform
Swedish General Election: no party wins a majority with far-right Sweden Democrats making gains
Australia experiences its earliest and most severe start to the fire season after fighting dozens of bushfires in Queensland and New South Wales
John Legend and wifeChrissy Teigenfire back at Donald Trump on social media after he calls them "boring" and "filthymouthed" in tweets over Criminal Justice Reform
Nigerian government says it will repatriate 600 people from South Africa after two people killed in wave of xenophobic violence in Johannesburg
Scientist reveal evidence of humans earliest milk consumption, 6,000 years ago from the dental plaque of teeth of prehistoric farmers from Britain
Global death toll from COVID-19 passes 900,000 with the US the most deaths at 190,589
San Francisco Bay area blanketed by dark orange skies and smoke due to California wildfires
17 hospital patients die after heavy rainfall and flooding in Tula, central Mexico
US 2021 summer the hottest on record with average 74 degrees Fahrenheit, overtaking record set in 1936, during the Dust Bowl
G2 summit begins in New Delhi, India: African Union invited to become a permanent member and statement on war in Ukraine condemns use of force to grab territory
Fashion designer Sarah Burton, former head designer at Alexander McQueen, is named Givenchy's new Creative Director
Vietnam's Phong Chau bridge collapses into the Red River, in the wake of Super Typhoon Yagi, taking 10 cars and two scooters with it
World's first whole-eye and face transplant is declared successful for a 47-year-old Arkansas man more than a year after the operation
Battle of Flodden: English forces defeat the Scots near Branxton in Northumberland and kill KingJames IVof Scotland, the last monarch in Great Britain to be killed in battle
Mary Stuart, at nine months old, is crownedQueen of Scotsin the central Scottish town of Stirling
PopePaul IVrefuses to crown Ferdinand I of Austria Holy Roman Emperor
Conference of Poissy: Religious theologians gathered byCatherine de' Medici
Ralph Waldo Emersonpublishes his influential essay "Nature" in the US, outlining his beliefs in transcendentalism
English scientist and astronomerJohn Herscheltakes the first glass plate photograph
Robert E. Leesplits his army & sends Jackson to capture Harpers Ferry
French CaptainAlfred Dreyfussentenced on unjust grounds
Orville Wrightmakes the first hour-long airplane flight, lasting 62 minutes and 15 seconds at Fort Myer, Virginia
Jack Johnsonretains his heavyweight boxing title when he fights Al Kaufman to a no decision in 10 rounds at Coffroth's Arena, San Francisco, California
Hydrofoil designed byAlexander Graham Bell, his wife Mabel Bell and F.W. Casey Baldwin sets new water speed record of 114 km/h on Bras d'Or, Nova Scotia
Tony LazzeriDay at Yankee Stadium
Lieutenant-GeneralOmar Bradleyflies from Algiers to Marrakech/Prestwick

First "bug" in a computer program is discovered by a team of engineers, includingGrace Hopper, in Cambridge, Massachusetts, when a real-life moth is removed with tweezers from a relay and taped into the logbook
Jimmie Foxxhits his 534th and final home run
Elvis Presley's first appearance on "TheEd SullivanShow"
"Diana" byPaul Ankareaches #1
US PresidentEisenhowersigns 1st civil rights bill since Reconstruction
Alabama GovernorGeorge Wallaceserved a federal injunction to stop orders of state police to bar black students from enrolling in white schools
François Mitterrandnominated for French presidency
LA Dodgers future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherSandy Koufaxthrows his 4th career no-hitter and first perfect game in a 1-0 win over the Chicago Cubs at Dodger Stadium
The National Traffic and Motor Vehicle Safety Act signed into law by U.S. PresidentLyndon B. Johnson, 1st federal safety standards for vehicles and roads
MLB CommissionerBowie Kuhnsuspends Detroit Tigers pitcherDenny McLainfor carrying a gun
Apple Records releasesJohn Lennon's second solo studio album, "Imagine" in US; it tops the charts in US, UK, Australia, and 3 other countries
4th place finish in the Italian Grand Prix at Monza is enough to clinchJackie Stewarthis 3rd Formula 1 World Drivers Championship
Paul McCartney& Wings begin their "Wings Over The World" tour in Southampton, England; 65 concerts in Europe, Australia, Canada, and United States, runs through October of 1976
Ayatollah Khomeinicalls for an uprising in the Iranian army
British film "The Bitch" starringJoan Collins, a sequel to "The Stud", both based on novels by her sisterJackie Collins, premieres in UK
President Reaganorders sanctions against South Africa, targeting apartheid
Larry Birdof the Celtics begins an NBA free throw streak of 59
MLB pitcherNolan Ryanstrikes out his 4,500th batter
Mike Tysonindicted for rape of Desiree Washington
"Band of Brothers" based on book byStephen E. Ambrose, created bySteven SpielbergandTom Hankspremieres, then most expensive miniseries ever made

Two al-Qaeda linked suicide bombers disguised as journalists kill Northern Alliance leaderAhmad Shah Massoudby detonating explosives hidden in a camera and a battery-pack belt while interviewing him in Takhar Province, northeastern Afghanistan
A court in the Philippines ordersImelda Marcosto repay the government almost $280,000 for funds taken from the National Food Authority byFerdinand Marcosin 1983
Queen Elizabeth IIbecomes Great Britain's longest-reigning monarch at 63 years and seven months, beating the previous record set by her great-great-grandmother,Queen Victoria

PoetJohn Milton's own copy ofShakespeare's First Folio of 1623 has survived with his annotations according to scholar Jason Scott-Warren in Philadelphia library, could be world's most important modern literary discovery
Donald Trumppurposely downplayed the pandemic in early 2020 to avoid panic according toBob Woodward's new book "Rage"
Tom Bradybecomes first player in NFL history to start 300 regular season games as he guides Tampa Bay Buccaneers to an opening day 31-29 win at home to Dallas Cowboys
US PresidentJoe Bidenannounces widespread COVID-19 vaccine mandates for federal workers, contractors and large employers affecting 100 million people
Catherine, Princess of Wales, reveals she has completed her preventive chemotherapy treatment in a video message
Battle of Svolder, Baltic Sea: King Olaf on board the Long Serpent is defeated in one of the greatest naval battles of the Viking Age and leaps to his death overboard
Easter Island / Rapa Nui in the Pacific is annexed by Chile
